tasks.description: Define own types in `src/tools/video-editor/types/timeline-canvas.ts` to replace all `@xzdarcy/timeline-engine` types: `TimelineAction`, `TimelineRow`, `TimelineEffect`, and `TimelineCanvasHandle` (replaces `TimelineState`). Update all 11 importing files to use the new types. Run `tsc --noEmit` to confirm zero xzdarcy type references remain in video-editor source.
tasks.executor_notes: Added `src/tools/video-editor/types/timeline-canvas.ts` with local `TimelineAction`, `TimelineRow`, `TimelineEffect`, and `TimelineCanvasHandle` definitions. Migrated the 11 video-editor files that imported xzdarcy timeline types to the new local types. Verification: `rg -l "@/tools/video-editor/types/timeline-canvas" src/tools/video-editor | sort` lists the 11 migrated files, `rg -n "import type .*@xzdarcy|from '@xzdarcy/timeline-engine'|TimelineState.*@xzdarcy/react-timeline-editor" src/tools/video-editor` returns no matches, and `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` exits 0.

tasks.description: Build `<TimeRuler>` component at `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimeRuler.tsx`. Accepts `scale`, `scaleWidth`, `scaleSplitCount`, `startLeft` (20px), `scrollLeft`, `totalWidth`. Renders major ticks with `formatTime` labels, minor ticks at subdivisions. Handles click-to-set-time and pointer-drag-to-scrub on the ruler area, calling `onClickTimeArea(time)` and `onCursorDrag(time)`. Style with Tailwind using existing dark theme tokens. Ruler syncs horizontal scroll via controlled `scrollLeft` prop (not its own scrollbar).
tasks.executor_notes: Added `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimeRuler.tsx` as a controlled ruler component. It renders major ticks at `scaleWidth` intervals, minor ticks at `scaleWidth / scaleSplitCount`, translates content by `scrollLeft` instead of owning a scrollbar, and maps pointer positions to time with `(clientX - rect.left + scrollLeft - startLeft) / pixelsPerSecond`, clamped to the ruler extent. Click and scrub are separated by a small pointer-move threshold. Labels use the shared `coordinate-utils.formatTime` helper, matching the existing playback formatter logic. Verification: `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` exits 0.

tasks.description: Build `<TimelineCanvas>` grid shell at `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.tsx`. Scrollable container rendering rows at `ROW_HEIGHT` (36px) with actions positioned absolutely by `start * pixelsPerSecond + startLeft` / `(end - start) * pixelsPerSecond`. Accepts `getActionRender(action, row)` for custom clip content. Native `overflow: auto` scrolling. Background grid lines via repeating-linear-gradient. Expose `TimelineCanvasHandle` via `useImperativeHandle`: `setTime(t)` updates cursor position via ref (not state) for perf during playback, `getTime()`, and `target` (grid DOM node). Render cursor line as absolute-positioned div. Compose `<TimeRuler>` above the grid with shared horizontal scroll sync via onScroll callback. CRITICAL: Also sync vertical scroll between the grid container and the external track-label pane — expose an `onScroll` callback or accept a `trackLabelRef` to keep them aligned. The current layout has track labels in a separate sibling container.
tasks.executor_notes: Added `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.tsx` as the custom grid shell. It computes `pixelsPerSecond = scaleWidth / scale`, positions actions at `startLeft + action.start * pixelsPerSecond`, sizes them with `(action.end - action.start) * pixelsPerSecond`, derives timeline width from clamped scale-count math, composes `TimeRuler` with shared `scrollLeft` state, exposes a `.timeline-canvas-edit-area` scroll container, and syncs `scrollTop` into an optional `trackLabelRef`. The forwarded `TimelineCanvasHandle.setTime()` path updates the cursor line imperatively through `syncCursor()` rather than React state. Verification: `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` exits 0.

tasks.description: Add pointer-event resize handles to each action wrapper in `<TimelineCanvas>`. Left and right thin (8px) absolute-positioned divs with `cursor: ew-resize`. On pointerdown: capture pointer, record initial start/end and clientX, fire `onActionResizeStart({action, row, dir})`. On pointermove: compute new start or end from delta, clamp >= 0 and enforce minimum width, update TRANSIENT local action geometry in TimelineCanvas state so the clip visually resizes during drag, and fire `onActionResizing({action, row, dir})`. On pointerup: fire `onActionResizeEnd({action, row, dir})` and clear transient state. CRITICAL: TimelineCanvas must own a transient override map (e.g. `resizingOverrides: Map<actionId, {start, end}>`) that takes precedence over prop-driven positions during active resize. Without this, clips will snap rather than smoothly resize. The callback signatures must match what `useClipResize.ts` expects so it requires zero changes.
tasks.executor_notes: Updated `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.tsx` with left/right 8px pointer resize handles on each action wrapper. The component now owns transient resize geometry in `resizeOverrides`, mirrored in `resizePreviewRef` so pointerup uses the latest drag position without waiting for React state. Pointerdown captures the handle pointer, fires `onActionResizeStart({ action, row, dir })`, pointermove updates the transient geometry and fires `onActionResizing({ action, row, start, end, dir })`, and pointerup fires `onActionResizeEnd({ action, row, start, end, dir })` before clearing the override. This preserves `useClipResize.ts` callback compatibility without any hook changes. Verification: `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` exits 0.

tasks.description: Update `src/tools/video-editor/lib/drop-position.ts` DOM queries. Replace `.timeline-editor-edit-area` with a stable class `.timeline-canvas-edit-area` (or ref from TimelineCanvas). Replace `.ReactVirtualized__Grid` scroll queries with the grid container's own scrollLeft/scrollTop (same element). Update `getTimelineDomNodes` WeakMap cache and validation. Also update `TimelineEditor.tsx` `clientXToTime` helper (lines ~116-121) that queries similar selectors. Verify `useClipDrag`, `useExternalDrop`, and `useDragCoordinator` still compute correct positions after the selector changes.
tasks.executor_notes: Updated `src/tools/video-editor/lib/drop-position.ts` and `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineEditor.tsx` to resolve the new `.timeline-canvas-edit-area` first and treat that scroll container as the authoritative grid for `scrollLeft`/`scrollTop`. The lookup logic still falls back to `.timeline-editor-edit-area` and `.ReactVirtualized__Grid` so the current library-backed editor keeps working until T6 swaps components. `getTimelineDomNodes()` cache validation now checks `isConnected` and `wrapper.contains(node)` before reusing cached nodes, so DOM swaps invalidate stale references correctly. Verification: `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` exits 0.

tasks.description: Wire `<TimelineCanvas>` into `TimelineEditor.tsx`, replacing the `<Timeline>` import and JSX. Pass: `ref={timelineRef}`, `rows` (drop the `immovableRows` memo since `movable` was only for the library), `scale`, `scaleWidth`, `scaleSplitCount`, `startLeft`, `rowHeight`, `minScaleCount`, `maxScaleCount`, `getActionRender`, `onCursorDrag`, `onClickTimeArea`, `onActionResizeStart`, `onActionResizeEnd`. Wire vertical scroll sync between the track-label container and TimelineCanvas grid. Remove `immovableRows` memo. Remove `@xzdarcy/react-timeline-editor` import and CSS import. Remove `effects` prop if unused by TimelineCanvas (the `effects` map was only consumed by the library). Verify `onChange` path: the library's `onChange` fired during resize to update rows — with custom resize, `onActionResizeEnd` in `useClipResize` handles the final edit directly, so `onChange` callback can be removed.
tasks.executor_notes: Replaced the library `<Timeline>` usage in `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineEditor.tsx` with `<TimelineCanvas>`, passing the forwarded `timelineRef`, `rows`, scale props, renderer, playback callbacks, and resize callbacks. The track-label pane now keeps a `trackListRef` and pushes its own `scrollTop` into `timelineRef.current?.setScrollTop(...)`, while `TimelineCanvas` continues to push grid `scrollTop` back into that same ref for alignment. The `immovableRows` memo, `effects` prop, and library `onChange` path were removed from the editor swap. `useClipEditing.ts`, `useTimelineState.ts`, and `useTimelineState.types.ts` were updated so the old resize-only `onChange` callback is no longer surfaced through editor context. Verification: `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` exits 0, and `rg` confirms `TimelineEditor.tsx` now imports `TimelineCanvas` and no longer references `@xzdarcy/react-timeline-editor`, `immovableRows`, or editor-context `onChange`.

tasks.description: Migrate CSS: remove `@xzdarcy/react-timeline-editor/dist/react-timeline-editor.css` import. Update `timeline-overrides.css` — remove all rules targeting `.timeline-editor-*` classes that no longer exist (header, edit-row, action styles). Keep custom styles: `.cross-track-ghost`, `.drop-indicator-*`, `[data-drag-over]` glow, and drop-target pseudo-elements. Re-target the `.timeline-editor-edit-area.drop-target-new-track::after` rule to use `.timeline-canvas-edit-area.drop-target-new-track::after`. Add any new styles needed for TimelineCanvas/TimeRuler if not fully handled by Tailwind.
tasks.executor_notes: Updated `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/timeline-overrides.css` to remove obsolete `.timeline-editor-*` rules from the old library and keep only the drag/drop affordances still used by the custom canvas. The external drag-over glow, cross-track ghost, and drop-indicator classes remain intact. The `drop-target-new-track::after` pseudo-element was retargeted from `.timeline-editor-edit-area` to `.timeline-canvas-edit-area`, with `pointer-events: none` preserved for overlay safety. No extra TimelineCanvas/TimeRuler CSS was required beyond this retargeting because the new shell is styled via Tailwind. Verification: `rg` shows no remaining `.timeline-editor-*` selectors in `timeline-overrides.css`, and `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` exits 0.

tasks.description: Remove the library: `npm uninstall @xzdarcy/react-timeline-editor`. Verify zero remaining imports with `grep -r 'xzdarcy' src/`. Verify `react-virtualized` is also gone from node_modules (it's only a transitive dep — confirmed no direct usage elsewhere). Run `tsc --noEmit` and `npm run build` to confirm clean compilation. Check bundle size delta with `npx vite-bundle-visualizer` or compare build output sizes.
tasks.executor_notes: Removed `@xzdarcy/react-timeline-editor` from `package.json` and `package-lock.json` via `npm uninstall`. Verification: `grep -R "xzdarcy" src/` returns no matches, `rg -n "xzdarcy|react-virtualized" package.json package-lock.json` returns no matches, `npm ls @xzdarcy/react-timeline-editor react-virtualized` reports an empty tree, and `test -d node_modules/react-virtualized && echo present || echo missing` reports `missing`. The lockfile diff shows the xzdarcy subtree and transitive `interactjs`/`react-virtualized` entries were removed. Build gates passed with `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` and `npm run build`; the production build emitted final asset sizes including `dist/assets/index-CHiZ4DHk.js` at `3513.08 kB` minified / `960.12 kB` gzip. Best-effort bundle-size conclusion: the removed dependency subtree strongly indicates the expected reduction, but a direct >=200 kB minified delta was not measured because no local visualizer or pre-removal baseline was available.
commands_run: test -d node_modules/react-virtualized && echo present || echo missing

tasks.description: End-to-end manual validation of all 12 interaction paths: (1) click ruler to set playhead, (2) drag cursor on ruler to scrub, (3) resize clip from left edge, (4) resize clip from right edge, (5) internal clip drag within track, (6) cross-track clip drag with floating ghost, (7) external file drop creating skeleton, (8) external asset drop from sidebar, (9) drop below all tracks to create new track, (10) split clip via context menu, (11) delete clip via context menu, (12) playback sync between timeline cursor and Remotion preview. Also test zoom if scaleWidth is adjustable. Verify no regressions in drop indicators, ghost rendering, and cursor tracking.

watch_items: RESIZE TRANSIENT STATE (flag: resize-preview-path-missing): TimelineCanvas MUST own transient action geometry during resize drag. The library previously handled live visual updates internally via onChange. Without a local override map in TimelineCanvas, clips will snap to final position instead of smoothly resizing. useClipResize only does start/end bookkeeping — it does NOT drive visual updates during drag.

VERTICAL SCROLL SYNC (flag: virtualization-and-scroll-sync-regression): Track labels and timeline grid are in separate sibling containers (left sidebar 144px fixed, right flex-1). The library's internal scroll handled this implicitly. TimelineCanvas must expose vertical scroll position so the track-label pane stays aligned. Test with enough tracks to overflow vertically.

DROP POSITION DOM QUERIES: drop-position.ts uses WeakMap-cached queries for `.timeline-editor-edit-area` and `.ReactVirtualized__Grid` to get scroll offsets during drop calculations. These must be updated atomically with the component swap — if selectors break, all drag-drop operations fail silently or miscalculate positions.
HORIZONTAL SCROLL SYNC: TimeRuler must scroll in lockstep with the grid body. The library handled this internally. Use a shared onScroll callback — do not give the ruler its own scrollbar.

onChange REMOVAL: The library's onChange fired during resize to update rows via buildRowTrackPatches. With custom resize, useClipResize.onActionResizeEnd handles the final edit directly. Verify the onChange callback from useClipEditing.ts is truly only needed for resize — if it served other purposes, removing it will break things.

EFFECTS MAP: timeline-data.ts imports TimelineEffect and builds an effects map. Verify whether TimelineCanvas needs this at all — the library used it for action type rendering, but getActionRender already provides custom rendering. May be safely droppable.

50+ TRACK PERFORMANCE: Virtualization is deferred. If Step 9 testing reveals poor performance with many tracks, this becomes a blocking follow-up before merge. Native scroll with 50+ rows of absolute-positioned action divs may cause jank.

AUTOSCROLL DURING DRAG: The library provided autoScroll prop. Verify useClipDrag's existing scroll logic handles auto-scrolling independently. If the library was contributing to scroll-during-drag behavior, it must be replicated.

CSS CLASS MIGRATION: Several custom styles target `.timeline-editor-edit-area` (e.g., drop-target-new-track::after pseudo-element). All must be retargeted to `.timeline-canvas-edit-area` or equivalent.

sense_checks.question: Do all 11 files compile with zero imports from @xzdarcy/* after the type migration? Is TimelineEffect included (timeline-data.ts imports it)?
sense_checks.executor_note: All 11 type-importing files now compile against the new local timeline-canvas types, and `timeline-data.ts` uses the local `TimelineEffect`. Zero xzdarcy type imports remain under `src/tools/video-editor`. The only remaining `@xzdarcy` references are the existing Timeline component and CSS value imports in `TimelineEditor.tsx`, which are handled in later batches.

sense_checks.question: Does the ruler render ticks at correct intervals and produce accurate time values from click/drag interactions? Does formatTime output match the previous ruler's label format?
sense_checks.executor_note: The ruler spacing is derived directly from `scaleWidth`, `scale`, and `scaleSplitCount`, while click/drag time conversion is implemented as `(clientX - rect.left + scrollLeft - startLeft) / pixelsPerSecond` with clamping to the available width. Major labels use the shared `coordinate-utils.formatTime` helper, which matches the current playback formatter logic. The component compiles cleanly; visual/manual validation will follow when T3 wires it into the editor.

sense_checks.question: Do actions position correctly at `start * pixelsPerSecond + startLeft` with correct width? Does horizontal scroll sync between ruler and grid work? Does the cursor line update smoothly during playback (via ref, not state)?
sense_checks.executor_note: Action placement and sizing are implemented directly with `startLeft + action.start * pixelsPerSecond` and `(action.end - action.start) * pixelsPerSecond`. Horizontal scroll sync is driven by shared `scrollLeft` state passed from the scroll container into `TimeRuler`. Cursor updates use the imperative `syncCursor()` path called by `setTime()` on the forwarded handle, so playback updates do not depend on React state. The component compiles cleanly; visual/manual confirmation will happen once T6 mounts it in the editor.

sense_checks.question: Does the clip visually resize smoothly during drag (not snap on release)? Does the transient override map clear correctly on pointerup? Do useClipResize callbacks receive the correct action/row/dir values without any changes to useClipResize.ts?
sense_checks.executor_note: The canvas now keeps active resize geometry in a transient override map that is consulted during rendering, so clips resize live instead of snapping on release. The override is cleared on pointerup/pointercancel, and the final callback uses the latest geometry from a ref-backed preview cache. Resize callbacks still use the same `{ action, row, dir }` core shape expected by `useClipResize.ts`, with extra `start/end` fields matching the former library contract.

sense_checks.question: Do internal clip drags and external drops land at the correct time position and track after the DOM query migration? Does the WeakMap cache in drop-position.ts correctly invalidate when nodes change?
sense_checks.executor_note: Drop-position lookups now prefer `.timeline-canvas-edit-area` and use that same element for scroll offsets, while still falling back to the legacy `.timeline-editor-edit-area`/`.ReactVirtualized__Grid` path until T6. `TimelineEditor.clientXToTime()` was updated with the same selector order, and `getTimelineDomNodes()` now invalidates stale cached nodes when the wrapper DOM changes. The shared `computeDropPosition()` path used by drag coordinator and external drop still compiles cleanly with the migrated selectors.

sense_checks.question: Does the full editor load with clips at correct positions? Does vertical scroll sync keep track labels aligned with timeline rows? Is the immovableRows memo and onChange callback safely removed without side effects?
sense_checks.executor_note: The editor now mounts `TimelineCanvas` directly, so clip positioning comes from the new canvas path rather than the library. Vertical alignment is wired both ways through `trackListRef` and `timelineRef.setScrollTop(...)`, and the old `immovableRows` plus library `onChange` resize path were removed from the editor/context surface. This batch was verified by compile-time checks and code-path inspection; full runtime/manual confirmation of loaded clip positions and scroll alignment remains part of T9.

sense_checks.question: Are drop indicators, ghost clips, drag-over glow, and 'drop to create track' pseudo-element all rendering correctly with the retargeted CSS selectors?
sense_checks.executor_note: The override sheet now only targets selectors still present in the custom editor flow: cross-track ghost, drop indicators, drag-over glow, and the `drop-target-new-track` pseudo-element on `.timeline-canvas-edit-area`. No legacy `.timeline-editor-*` selectors remain in `timeline-overrides.css`. This batch was verified by selector inspection and compile-time checks; final runtime rendering confirmation of those affordances remains part of T9.

sense_checks.question: Is `@xzdarcy/react-timeline-editor` gone from package.json and node_modules? Is `react-virtualized` also removed? Does `npm run build` succeed? Is bundle size reduction >= 200KB minified?
sense_checks.executor_note: `@xzdarcy/react-timeline-editor` is gone from `package.json`, `package-lock.json`, and `node_modules`, and `react-virtualized` is also no longer installed. `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` and `npm run build` both succeed. Direct confirmation of a >=200 kB minified bundle reduction was not possible in this environment because `vite-bundle-visualizer` is not installed and there is no recorded pre-removal build baseline; best-effort evidence is the lockfile removal of the xzdarcy/interactjs/react-virtualized subtree plus the absence of `@xzdarcy/react-timeline-editor`, `react-virtualized`, and `interactjs` strings in `dist/assets`.

sense_checks.question: Do all 12 interaction paths pass? Specifically: does resize feel smooth (not snappy), does cross-track drag show the floating ghost, does external drop create skeletons correctly, and does playback sync stay frame-accurate?
sense_checks.executor_note: I cannot honestly mark all 12 interaction paths as passed from this environment because there is no browser automation harness and manual browser interaction is unavailable; a local Vite server startup attempt is blocked by sandbox `EPERM` on `listen`. Best-effort evidence is positive but indirect: targeted video-editor tests pass (6 files / 15 tests), the build/typecheck gates are already green, and code inspection shows the new timeline implementation is wired for smooth resize, cross-track ghost rendering, external-drop skeleton creation, new-track affordances, and playback-driven cursor sync. Final pass/fail for SC9 still requires an interactive browser session outside this sandbox.

meta_commentary:
EXECUTION GUIDANCE:

**The two hardest parts of this plan are T3 (grid shell) and T4 (resize handles). Get these right and everything else follows.**

**Resize transient state (most likely source of bugs):** The library previously owned action geometry and updated it internally during resize drags. Our useClipResize hook only records start position on resize-start and commits the final edit on resize-end — it does NOT drive visual updates during the drag. TimelineCanvas must maintain a `resizingOverrides` map (actionId → {start, end}) that overrides prop-driven positions while a resize is active. During pointermove, update this map AND re-render the affected action at its new width. On pointerup, clear the override and let the committed data flow back through props. If you skip this, clips will appear frozen during resize and then jump to their final position.

**Scroll sync (second most likely regression):** The current layout has track labels in a fixed-width (144px) left sidebar and the Timeline in a flex-1 right pane. These are sibling containers. The library's internal scroll handled vertical alignment implicitly because track labels were outside its scope — BUT the track-label container has its own vertical overflow. You need to wire an onScroll handler that keeps the track-label pane's scrollTop in sync with the grid's scrollTop (or vice versa). Test this with enough tracks to require scrolling.

**onChange removal safety:** Verify by reading useClipEditing.ts carefully. The onChange handler calls `applyTimelineEdit(nextRows, buildRowTrackPatches(nextRows))`. During library-managed resize, this was the path that updated row data. With custom resize, useClipResize.onActionResizeEnd calls its own applyTimelineEdit. Double-check there's no other code path that triggers onChange.

**DOM query migration (T5) must be done before or simultaneously with T6.** If you swap in TimelineCanvas while drop-position.ts still queries `.timeline-editor-edit-area`, all drops will silently fail or miscalculate. Consider adding the `.timeline-canvas-edit-area` class to the grid container in T3 so T5 can be validated independently.

**pixelsPerSecond calculation:** In the library, `pixelsPerSecond = scaleWidth / scale`. Verify this formula is correct by checking coordinate-utils or the library source. All action positioning depends on this being right.

**Testing approach for T9:** Don't just check that things work — check that positions are CORRECT. Create a clip, note its from/to times, verify the clip's pixel position matches `time * pixelsPerSecond + startLeft`. Resize a clip and verify the metadata update matches the visual. This catches off-by-one-startLeft errors.

**Single PR:** User wants one PR, all in one go. But develop incrementally — you can render TimelineCanvas alongside Timeline temporarily during T2-T3 for visual comparison before the swap in T6.
